title: How to divide monthly salary between rent, EMI, SIP, insurance and savings?
summary: Effective management of monthly salary is crucial for financial stability. The 50/30/20 rule allocates income into essential expenses (50%), lifestyle spending (30%), and savings (20%), promoting disciplined budgeting, investment, and insurance coverage for long-term wealth building.

title: ITR filing 2026: Why waiting until June 15 could help taxpayers avoid notices
summary: The ITR filing deadline for FY 2025-26 is July 31, 2026. Tax experts advise against filing before June 15 to ensure all documents are updated, as early submissions may lead to discrepancies and incorrect reporting in certain cases. Details here.

title: FD laddering strategy: How to split ₹10 lakh for better returns and liquidity
summary: FD laddering involves spreading investments across multiple fixed deposits with varying maturities, enhancing liquidity and flexibility. Here's how it works and how the strategy benefits investors when repo rate is changed by RBI.

title: I asked ChatGPT: Which city should I retire to in 2040? How much should I save? AI details how much corpus is needed
summary: ChatGPT suggests a 3.25% withdrawal rate for early retirement in India, requiring an investment of ₹10-17 crore, depending on the city. It recommends maximising earnings before transitioning to lower-cost cities.

title: Senior citizen FD vs SCSS: Which gives better monthly income after retirement?
summary: Senior citizens can choose between FDs and SCSS for income post-retirement. SCSS often yields higher returns, whereas FDs offer flexibility in contributions and payout structures. Full comparison here.&nbsp;

title: Planning FD? Here's how much you should invest to earn ₹10,000 monthly interest — see calculation
summary: Fixed deposits are popular for stable income, particularly among retirees. Banks provide various payout options, and non-cumulative FDs offer periodic interest payments. Here's how much investment is needed to earn ₹10,000 monthly income.

title: SCSS account rules: Who can invest, how much interest you get and what tax applies
summary: The Senior Citizens Savings Scheme (SCSS) offers Indian retirees a secure investment with guaranteed returns. Eligible individuals can invest a minimum of ₹1,000, with a maximum limit of ₹30 lakh. The scheme provides attractive quarterly interest payouts and tax benefits under Section 80C.
